steps.title: Identify the appeal
steps.content: The underlined reference says readers of Brown's biography would bear the author out in his statement. The author invokes people familiar with Brown's life to support his own respectful and admiring judgment of her.

steps.title: Separate unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A overstates the point by claiming Brown's life was popular with writers. Choice C concerns why Brown was unique, not what the biography reference does. Choice D concerns Yung's relationship with Brown, which the reference does not describe. The answer is B.
